purpose: Load, validate, index, and query local JSON manifest files from a directory for capability discovery and project metadata lookups.

endpoints.description: Load and validate manifest entries from a local directory.
Search loaded manifest entries by capability label.

sidecar_specification.purpose: Local JSON manifest registry loader and capability index.

agent_usage.when_to_use: Use when a system stores manifest JSON files on disk and needs offline discovery by capability or metadata.
agent_usage.when_not_to_use: Do not use for remote package registries, SQL-backed catalogs, or high-scale distributed search.
agent_usage.example_workflow: 1) Load a directory of manifest JSON files. 2) Validate and index them. 3) Search by capability or inspect the loaded entries.

common_calls.description: Search a local registry by capability

failure_modes.condition: Manifest file is invalid JSON or fails schema validation
failure_modes.symptom: File is skipped and reported in skipped_files
failure_modes.recovery: Fix the manifest file and reload the registry

forbidden_behaviors.description: Must not write outside declared scope
Must not make external network calls

agent_notes.implementation_hint: Read .json files from a configured directory, validate each manifest through a pluggable validator, keep valid entries in memory, and allow simple capability-based filtering.
agent_notes.known_gotchas: Manifest directories often contain partial or invalid files during editing. The loader should skip invalid entries without crashing the whole registry.
agent_notes.preferred_approach: Separate file system loading from manifest validation so other manifest schemas can reuse the registry later.
agent_notes.avoid_approach: Do not recursively scan unrelated directories and do not assume every JSON file is a valid manifest.
